vitals.field_size: 3,700+ road riders across the 5 L'Etape Denmark road routes
vitals.feed_zones: 7 feed stations on the 300 km route
vitals.cutoff_time: 20:00 finish closure — roughly 12.5-13.5 hours depending on start wave

climate.description: Mild Nordic summer with long daylight hours; variable weather typical of Jutland peninsula.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: The full route starts on Flensburg's waterfront, crosses the German-Danish border at Rønsdam after roughly 9 km, and follows the historic Hærvejen corridor north along Jutland's glacial ridge. Open South Jutland countryside gradually gives way to the rolling hills of Vejle Ådal, the Jelling monuments, forests around Vrads, and the glacial terrain of Dollerup Bakker. It's an endurance course rather than an alpine gran fondo — 2,500 m of climbing is accumulated through repeated short rollers over 300 km, not one dominant pass. The road component is now co-branded L'Etape Denmark by Tour de France (only the 5 road routes carry L'Etape status; MTB and gravel remain plain Hærvejsløbet), with genuine timed yellow/green/polka-dot jersey competitions. Roads are open or only partially closed — riders must obey normal traffic law.
course_description.signature_challenge: The sheer distance and cumulative rolling terrain over 300 km rather than any single climb — four named KOM climbs are spread across the route, with the final one only ~12 km from the Viborg finish.
suffering_zones: Côte du Hjarupvej (km 41) — 450 m at ~10% average, the first timed KOM
Mur de Refsgårde (km 175) — 550 m at 8.2% average, arriving as the route enters the hillier Vejle Ådal
Passo del Vrads (km 242) — 1.3 km at 5.2% average, difficult mainly because it lands after 8+ hours for many riders
Alpe d'Dollerup (km 289) — 1.3 km at 3.8% average, the fourth KOM and final serious climb, only ~12 km from Viborg

logistics.lodging_strategy: Viborg offers hotels, hostels, and barracks-area camping; book early for June event. Flensburg pre-race stays near start.
logistics.transport: Mass-participation gran fondo with 15 routes converging on Viborg's Paradepladsen; road, MTB, and gravel options. Queen stage starts Flensburg with chip timing before finish-line parade.

history.origin_story: DGI staged a roughly 50-rider test ride in 2012, then launched the public event with 850 participants in 2013, inspired by the ancient Hærvejen migration route used by Vikings, pilgrims, and armies. The 'since 1985' claim on file is unsupported and appears wrong — 2026 was the 14th edition, consistent with a 2013 launch, not 1985. The road routes are now co-branded L'Etape Denmark by Tour de France.
notable_moments: 2026: 14th edition, co-branded L'Etape Denmark, 3,700+ road riders across five distances

biased_opinion.verdict: Denmark's Biggest Ride, Honest About What It Is
biased_opinion.summary: Hærvejsløbet is DGI's mass-participation cycling weekend through Jutland, and its road routes now carry official L'Etape Denmark by Tour de France status — timed jerseys, priority L'Etape du Tour registration access, the works. That's a real ASO partnership, not borrowed branding. But it's co-branding, not a full rebrand: only the five road distances belong to L'Etape; the MTB and gravel events (also part of the same weekend, all converging on Viborg) stay under the plain Hærvejsløbet name, which makes the naming feel inconsistent depending on which page you land on. The flagship 300 km route is brutally long — 2,500 m of climbing sounds modest for the distance, and it is: this is a distance-and-wind test with four modest KOM climbs, not an alpine L'Etape. It also runs on open or only partially closed roads, a real gap from the closed-road experience the Tour branding might suggest, and the 'since 1985' founding date on file doesn't hold up — DGI's own history points to a 2012 test ride and 2013 launch.
strengths: Genuine L'Etape Denmark by Tour de France status on the road routes — timed jersey competitions and priority access to L'Etape du Tour registration
One of Denmark's largest recurring mass-participation cycling events, with roughly 1,000 volunteers and strong club support
A distinctive international, point-to-point route built around the historic Hærvejen corridor rather than an interchangeable circuit
Seven varied, club-operated feed stations on the 300 km route
weaknesses: Roads are open or only partially closed despite the Tour de France branding — riders must obey normal traffic law and may encounter other traffic
The L'Etape identity applies only to the road routes; Hærvejsløbet also covers MTB, gravel, and family events, and the naming can look inconsistent across pages
International, point-to-point format creates real transport logistics — riders finish 300 km from the Flensburg start, with bus and baggage transport handled separately
At 2,500 m over 300 km, it's brutally long but not especially mountainous — riders expecting an alpine-style L'Etape may find the challenge is distance and wind, not major climbs
The 'founded 1985' claim previously on file is unsupported; DGI's own history points to a 2012 test and 2013 public launch
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A genuinely massive, well-organized Danish cycling weekend with real L'Etape road-route status — go in expecting a long, rolling, open-road endurance test through Jutland's countryside, not an alpine climbing spectacle.

final_verdict.one_liner: Viking-fueled endurance test blending history, hills, and high-quality organization.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuine L'Etape-branded endurance ride through Jutland's countryside and don't need major mountain climbs. Expect open roads and a distance/wind test, not an alpine challenge.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an alpine L'Etape-branded event with genuine major climbs, look at other L'Etape Series events in mountainous regions (e.g., L'Etape France by Tour de France).

citations.label: Official 300 km Route Highlights and Climb Data

eligibility.notes: Co-branded L'Etape Denmark by Tour de France (road routes only, not a full rebrand of the MTB/gravel program); 2026 edition ran 27 June 2026 from Flensburg to Viborg with 3,700+ road riders confirmed. Vitals corrected to the actual 300km/2,500m route; founding date corrected from an unsupported 1985 to DGI's documented 2012 test ride/2013 public launch.
